name: social-content
description: When the user wants help creating, scheduling, or optimizing social media content for LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, or other platforms. Also use when the user mentions 'LinkedIn post,' 'Twitter thread,' 'social media,' 'content calendar,' 'social scheduling,' 'engagement,' or 'viral content.' This skill covers content creation, repurposing, and platform-specific strategies.
capabilities: Generate platform-specific post templates for LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook
Create content calendars and batching strategies for consistent posting
Develop effective hooks and CTAs based on proven engagement patterns
Repurpose long-form content (blogs, podcasts, videos) into social formats
Analyze and reverse-engineer viral content patterns in any niche
Provide platform-specific strategy tips based on algorithm preferences
author: coreyhaines31
securitySummary: All 35 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner misidentified markdown code block syntax (triple backticks) as shell backtick execution. The file is documentation-only (SKILL.md) with no executable code, network calls, or file system operations. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Users struggle to create consistent, platform-optimized social media content that drives engagement. This skill provides templates, strategies, and frameworks for creating posts across LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook.
limitations: Does not actually post or schedule content to social platforms
Cannot access real-time analytics or engagement data
Cannot scrape or fetch content from social platforms
Does not create visual assets (images, videos, graphics)

useCases.targetUser: Founders, consultants, and professionals building personal brand
useCases.title: Create a LinkedIn thought leadership post
useCases.description: Generate a story-based LinkedIn post following the proven format: hook, scene-setting, challenge, turning point, result, lesson, and engagement question.

useCases.targetUser: Marketing teams and solopreneurs managing multiple platforms
useCases.title: Build a weekly content calendar
useCases.description: Develop a structured weekly posting plan that balances different content pillars (educational, behind-the-scenes, engagement, promotional) across platforms.

useCases.title: Reverse engineer viral content in your niche
useCases.description: Apply the 6-step framework to identify top creators, extract proven patterns, and build a template library of hooks, formats, and CTAs that work.

promptTemplates.title: Basic social media post request
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a [platform] post about [topic]. Target audience: [description]. Goal: [awareness/engagement/leads]. Tone: [professional/casual/witty]. Include a hook and CTA.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick post generation with minimal context

promptTemplates.title: Full content brief
promptTemplates.prompt: I need help creating social content. Context: [brand description], [target audience], [current goals]. Create: [number] LinkedIn posts, [number] Twitter threads, and [number] Instagram captions for [timeframe]. Include content pillars and scheduling suggestions.

promptTemplates.title: Content repurposing
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a [blog post/podcast/video] about [topic]. Repurpose this into: 1) A LinkedIn carousel outline, 2) A Twitter thread (7 tweets), 3) An Instagram Reel script, 4) A TikTok short video concept.
promptTemplates.scenario: Turning long-form content into platform-specific pieces

promptTemplates.title: Viral pattern extraction
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me reverse-engineer viral content in the [industry] space. Walk me through: 1) How to identify top creators to study, 2) What metrics to track, 3) How to extract hook patterns, 4) How to build a reusable template library from findings.
promptTemplates.scenario: Learning from top performers in a specific niche

bestPractices: Always lead with a hook in the first line or first 3 seconds (this determines whether anyone reads the rest)
Adapt content to each platform's algorithm preferences and format constraints (e.g., no external links in LinkedIn posts)
Batch content creation in dedicated sessions (2-3 hours weekly) while leaving room for real-time engagement and trend responses
antiPatterns: Cross-posting identical content across platforms without adaptation (each platform has unique format and algorithm preferences)
Overly promotional content without providing value first (aim for 90% value, 10% promotion)
Scheduling everything with no real-time engagement (algorithms reward active participation in conversations)

faq.question: Does this skill actually post content to social media?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates content strategies, templates, and posts. You will need to copy the output to your social media scheduling tool or post directly.

faq.question: Can this skill access my social media analytics?
faq.answer: No, this skill cannot connect to social media APIs or access your account data. It provides frameworks and templates based on general best practices.

faq.question: Which platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook with platform-specific strategies, formats, and algorithm tips for each.

faq.question: How do I create viral content?
faq.answer: The skill includes a reverse engineering framework that guides you to study top performers in your niche, extract proven patterns, and build your own template library.

faq.question: Can I use this for client work or agency purposes?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill is suitable for creating content strategies and drafts for clients, though you will need to adapt the output to each client's specific voice and brand.

faq.question: How often should I post on each platform?
faq.answer: Recommended posting frequency: LinkedIn 3-5x per week, Twitter/X 3-10x daily (including replies), Instagram 1-2 posts + 3-10 Stories daily, TikTok 1-4x daily, Facebook 1-2x daily.
